request.question: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

prior_feedback.note: Switch trehalose to Sigma-Aldrich molecular biology grade (catalog T9531 family); avoid pharmaceutical-grade lots.
HeLa post-thaw recovery phase MUST be at least 48 hours, never 24 hours.

hypothesis.intervention: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium
hypothesis.threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control
hypothesis.mechanistic_rationale: due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures
hypothesis.readiness_rationale: All core fields are present and specific, making the hypothesis CRO-ready today.
hypothesis.literature_search_hint: trehalose sucrose cryoprotectant HeLa post-thaw viability
hypothesis.original_hypothesis: Replacing sucrose with trehalose as a cryoprotectant in the freezing medium will increase post-thaw viability of HeLa cells by at least 15 percentage points compared to the standard DMSO protocol, due to trehalose's superior membrane stabilization at low temperatures.

literature_qc.explanation: No retrieved papers specifically address the use of trehalose as a cryoprotectant in HeLa cells compared to DMSO. While some papers discuss trehalose and sucrose in the context of cryopreservation, they focus on sperm or other biological systems, which do not align with the specified biological system of HeLa cells.
literature_qc.recommended_action: No directly matching publication found. This combination appears novel. Proceed with experiment planning.

plan.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose versus DMSO as cryoprotectants.
plan.experimental_design: HeLa cells will be divided into two groups: one group will be cryopreserved using trehalose and the other using DMSO. Each group will undergo the same freezing and thawing protocols, followed by a 30-minute recovery period at 37°C before viability assessment using trypan blue exclusion. Each condition will be replicated three times to ensure statistical validity.
controls: HeLa cells cryopreserved with 10% DMSO in freezing medium.

step_by_step_protocol.description:
DAY 0 (PREP) | 30 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (w/v) trehalose solution by dissolving 1 g of trehalose (Sigma-Aldrich, T9531) in 10 mL of sterile PBS (pH 7.4). Filter sterilize using a 0.22 µm filter (Millipore, SLGP033RS).
Equipment: Pipettes, Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes, 0.22 µm filter
Reagents: 10% (w/v) trehalose solution in PBS
Expected outcome: Trehalose solution is sterile and clear with no particulate matter.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 15 min | Reagent Preparation
Prepare 10% (v/v) DMSO solution by diluting 1 mL of DMSO (Sigma-Aldrich, D8418) in 9 mL of sterile PBS. Store on ice until use.
Equipment: Pipettes, Sterile 50 mL centrifuge tubes
Reagents: 10% (v/v) DMSO in PBS
Expected outcome: DMSO solution is clear and well-mixed.
Safety: DMSO is a skin irritant; wear gloves.

DAY 0 (PREP) | 48 h | Cell Culture
Culture HeLa cells in DMEM (Gibco, 11965-092) supplemented with 10% FBS (Gibco, 26140-079) and 1% penicillin-streptomycin (Gibco, 15140-122) at 37°C with 5% CO2 until 80% confluence is reached (approximately 48 h).
Equipment: Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i), T25 flasks
Reagents: DMEM, 10% FBS, 1% penicillin-streptomycin
Expected outcome: Cells reach 80% confluence without contamination.

DAY 1 | 30 min | Cell Harvesting
Detach HeLa cells using 0.25% trypsin-EDTA (Gibco, 25200-056) for 5 min at 37°C. Neutralize with 10% FBS in DMEM, centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature, and resuspend in 1 mL of freezing medium (10% DMSO or 10% trehalose in DMEM).
Equipment: Centrifuge (Eppendorf, 5424), Pipettes, T25 flasks
Reagents: 0.25% trypsin-EDTA, 10% DMSO or 10% trehalose in DMEM
Expected outcome: Cells are detached, counted, and resuspended in freezing medium with >90% viability.

DAY 1 | 1 h | Cryopreservation
Aliquot 1 mL of cell suspension (1 x 10^6 cells/mL) into cryovials (Nunc, 368632). Place cryovials in a controlled-rate freezer (Thermo Scientific, Revco) and cool at -1°C/min to -80°C, then transfer to liquid nitrogen for storage.
Equipment: Controlled-rate freezer, Cryovials
Expected outcome: Cells are successfully frozen and stored in liquid nitrogen without crystallization.
Safety: Liquid nitrogen is hazardous; use appropriate PPE.

DAY 2 | 15 min | Thawing Cells
Retrieve cryovials from liquid nitrogen and immediately thaw in a 37°C water bath for 2-3 min until just thawed. Transfer to a 15 mL tube containing 9 mL of DMEM with 10% FBS to dilute DMSO or trehalose. Centrifuge at 300 × g for 5 min at room temperature.
Equipment: Water bath (Thermo Scientific), Centrifuge (Eppendorf, 5424), 15 mL tubes
Reagents: DMEM with 10% FBS
Expected outcome: Cells are thawed and diluted, with >90% recovery of viable cells.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Post-Thaw Recovery
Incubate the cells in DMEM with 10% FBS at 37°C for 30 min to allow recovery before viability assessment.
Equipment: Incubator (Thermo Scientific, Heracell 150i)
Expected outcome: Cells recover and stabilize post-thaw, ready for viability assessment.

DAY 2 | 30 min | Viability Assessment
Perform trypan blue exclusion assay by mixing 100 µL of cell suspension with 100 µL of 0.4% trypan blue (Gibco, 15250-061). Count viable cells using a hemocytometer (Hausser Scientific, 3100) under a microscope (Olympus, CKX41).
Equipment: Hemocytometer (Hausser Scientific, 3100), Microscope (Olympus, CKX41)
Reagents: 0.4% trypan blue solution
Expected outcome: Viability percentage calculated with >90% accuracy using hemocytometer.

DAY 2 | 1 h | Data Analysis
Calculate post-thaw viability percentages for both trehalose and DMSO groups. Use a two-tailed t-test to compare means between groups (α = 0.05).
Expected outcome: Statistical significance determined with p-value < 0.05, and viability of trehalose group at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.

DAY 2 | 3 days | Replication
Repeat the entire experiment (steps 1-10) two additional times to ensure reproducibility of results.
Expected outcome: Consistent results across all replicates, confirming the findings of the initial experiment.

assumptions: Trehalose will provide better cryoprotection than DMSO based on its membrane-stabilizing properties.
HeLa cells will survive the freezing and thawing process with both cryoprotectants.
The trypan blue exclusion method will accurately reflect cell viability.
decision_criteria: Proceed if post-thaw viability with trehalose is ≥ 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control.
Proceed if all replicates show consistent viability results within ±5% of the mean.
Stop if post-thaw viability with either treatment is < 50%.
reproducibility_notes: Use the same lot of trehalose and DMSO for all experiments.
Maintain consistent cell passage number (≤ 20 passages).
Calibrate the controlled-rate freezer before use.
feedback_incorporated:
PRIOR SCIENTIST CORRECTIONS FOR SIMILAR EXPERIMENTS:
A scientist has previously flagged these specific issues on similar comparative_cryopreservation experiments. You MUST implement each correction below in your output unless it directly contradicts the current hypothesis:
1. replicate gate in plan text
2. add 30-minute post-thaw recovery at 37°C before trypan blue counting
3. change one

validation.primary_endpoint: Post-thaw viability percentage at immediate and 24h checkpoints.
validation.success_threshold: at least 15 percentage points higher than DMSO control improvement over comparator in replicate-confirmed data.
failure_conditions: No meaningful viability gain in replicate-confirmed runs.
Intervention increases delayed viability loss at 24h.
validation.suggested_statistical_comparison: Two-group comparison against comparator using t-test or Mann-Whitney based on normality.
validation.minimum_replicates_or_design_note: Minimum three biological replicates before execution-level claim.

cro_ready_brief.objective: To compare the post-thaw viability of HeLa cells cryopreserved with trehalose versus DMSO as cryoprotectants.
scope_of_work: Execute pilot-first HeLa cryopreservation comparison between DMSO baseline and trehalose arm.
Deliver replicate-confirmed viability analysis and mitigation traceability report.
cro_ready_brief.sample_count: At least 3 biological replicates per arm
